pending.body:
finding_summary: `reconcile_failure()` is not actually idempotent for already-recorded failures if the replay arrives with a different `failed_at` or error message. In the `already_recorded` branch it still calls `repair_task_run_with_disposition` with the new `failed_at` ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:376-392]), and the final outcome/reason is recomputed from the new `error_summary` ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:469], [src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:1233-1247]). `repair_task_run_with_disposition` then feeds that timestamp into `repair_completion`, which can replace the existing `BeadFailed` journal event instead of treating the replay as a no-op ([src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2747-2807], [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1260-1278]). On an escalated third failure, replaying the same run with richer fallback text or a slightly different reconstructed timestamp will rewrite the operator-facing `NeedsOperator` reason and the durable failure timestamp, violating the bead's explicit idempotency requirement.
remediation_hint: For `already_recorded` replays, derive the canonical `failed_at` and canonical error summary from the recorded lineage/journal provenance before calling `repair_task_run_with_disposition` / `failure_outcome`, and add a regression test that replays the third failure with different message/timestamp inputs and asserts that both the milestone journal and controller transition journal stay unchanged.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 22 -> Plan and Implement
